# terrible_community_aws_ecs_taskdefinition (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `containers` (String) A list of containers definitions. See https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/ecs.html for a complete list of parameters.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `state` (String) State whether the task definition should exist or be deleted.

### Optional

- `arn` (String) The ARN of the task description to delete.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `cpu` (String) The number of cpu units used by the task. If *launch_type=EC2*, this field is optional and any value can be used. If *launch_type=FARGATE*, this field is required and you must use one of `256`, `512`, `1024`, `2048`, `4096`.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `execution_role_arn` (String) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the task execution role that the Amazon ECS container agent and the Docker daemon can assume.
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `family` (String) A Name that would be given to the task definition.
- `force_create` (Boolean) Always create new task definition.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `launch_type` (String) The launch type on which to run your task.
- `memory` (String) The amount (in MiB) of memory used by the task. If *launch_type=EC2*, this field is optional and any value can be used. If *launch_type=FARGATE*, this field is required and is limited by the CPU.
- `network_mode` (String) The Docker networking mode to use for the containers in the task. Windows containers must use *network_mode=default*, which will utilize docker NAT networking. Setting *network_mode=default* for a Linux container will use `bridge` mode.
- `placement_constraints` (String) Placement constraint objects to use for the task. You can specify a maximum of 10 constraints per task. Task placement constraints are not supported for tasks run on Fargate.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `revision` (Number) A revision number for the task definition.
- `runtime_platform` (String) runtime platform configuration for the task
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `task_role_arn` (String) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role that containers in this task can assume. All containers in this task are granted the permissions that are specified in this role.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `volumes` (String) A list of names of volumes to be attached.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `taskdefinition` (String) a reflection of the input parameters
